commit.message:
fix: disable GeneratePackageOnBuild to prevent parallel build race condition

When the full VS Code compound debug launch runs both TestSite and
MockBackOffice simultaneously, they both trigger a build of the shared
UmbracoPrism.Core project. With GeneratePackageOnBuild=true, one build
kicks off 'dotnet pack' while the other is mid-compile, causing a file
lock race and intermittent build failures.

The CI release pipeline already runs 'dotnet pack' explicitly, so this
flag provides no benefit in local development.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(client): prevent SPA router from intercepting blob URL downloads

The Produce Mobile download was causing a SecurityError because Umbraco's
SPA router was intercepting the programmatic anchor click and attempting
to navigate to the blob: URL using history.pushState().

fix: update mobileAppConfig.startUrl when Cloudflare tunnel hostname changes

The script updated prismTenants.hostname but not the startUrl field
inside the MobileAppConfig JSON blob. When produce-mobile ran after
the script, it used the stale startUrl from the previously saved config.

Now the UPDATE statement patches MobileAppConfig.startUrl via
json_set() in the same transaction, so the new tunnel URL is
immediately reflected when Produce Mobile is next run.

commit.message:
Fix relational operators failing for nullable IComparable types (e.g., Instant?) (#975)

* Initial plan

* Fix nullable IComparable types not working with relational operators (>, >=, <, <=)

When comparing two values of the same nullable type (e.g., Instant?) using relational
operators, the check for IComparable<> interface was done on the nullable type itself
(Nullable<T>), which doesn't directly implement IComparable<>. The fix uses
TypeHelper.GetNonNullableType() to get the underlying type first, so that if T
implements IComparable<T>, nullable T? also works correctly with relational operators.

Fixes: Operator '>' incompatible with operand types 'Instant?' and 'Instant?'

fix(contracts-gen): emit string/date-time schema for time.Time alias types

StartTime (type StartTime time.Time) and similar named alias types were
falling through to the generic struct branch and producing an empty-object
schema {}. Add a ConvertibleTo(time.Time) guard so any struct convertible
to time.Time emits {"type":"string","format":"date-time"}.

Also force-track cmd/contracts-gen/main.go with a .gitignore exception so
the generator source is preserved alongside its artifacts.

fields now carry the correct string schema.

Precise array shift invalidation for list mutations

Add shifted_from field to MutationTrie for range-based proxy
invalidation.  Mid-array insert, remove, pop, and slice operations now
only invalidate proxies at or after the shift point — earlier proxies
survive.

  end-of-array optimisation across Array and AoT arms

- stamp_shift on MutationTrie accumulates by taking min(threshold)
- stamp() clears shifted_from since revised_at subsumes it
- Slice deletion uses bump_shift(min_idx); slice assignment uses

fix: add staleness checks to KeysView, ValuesView, ItemsView

Views now store a creation revision and check the mutation trie before
every access, matching the existing ItemProxy staleness model.

Previously, accessing a view whose path had been replaced (e.g.
`view = doc["foo"].keys(); doc["foo"] = "bar"; list(view)`)
raised a confusing TypeError ("string does not support keys()").
Now it raises RuntimeError("stale"), consistent with how proxies
behave.

The staleness check is unified in Document.check_fresh(), used by both
ItemProxy and views, with a single consistent error message.

Root-level views (doc.keys()) remain live for child mutations since
the root path is never invalidated — only doc.clear() makes them stale.

commit.message:
fix: preserve exact coordinates entered in waypoint input fields (#392)

* fix: preserve exact coordinates entered in waypoint input fields

When a user types raw GPS coordinates (e.g. '34.129382,-118.141254')
into a waypoint field, the previous behaviour sent them to Nominatim
as a forward-geocode query, which snapped the result to the nearest
named address — sometimes more than a block away from the typed point.

Root cause: LRM's autocomplete fires a 'suggest' call as the user
types. The suggest results appear in a dropdown and get auto-selected.
When Enter is pressed, LRM uses that pre-selected suggest result
(with the Nominatim-snapped center) instead of calling geocode.

Fix: introduce a coord-preserving geocoder wrapper that detects
coordinate strings in both the geocode and suggest paths:

- If the input is coordinates, Nominatim reverse-geocodes the point
  for a human-readable display name, but the result's center is
  overridden with the exact typed lat/lon.
- If the input is a plain address, Nominatim forward-geocode is used
  as before.
- The wrapper bridges the library's Promise-based API to the
  callback-based API that leaflet-routing-machine expects.

Fixes #154

commit.message:
Add 12 platform-specific IPC examples (#64)

* Add file-watcher example with inotify-backed server and tests

Linux directory watcher using inotify that demonstrates IPC notifications.
Server watches directories and broadcasts FileChanged events to clients.
Seven integration tests cover watch/unwatch lifecycle, event notifications
for file creation/modification/deletion, and error handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add sysmon example with /proc-based CPU, memory, and load monitoring

Server reads /proc/stat, /proc/meminfo, and /proc/loadavg to implement
GetCpuUsage, GetMemoryInfo, and GetLoadAverage RPCs. A monitoring thread
checks thresholds every second and fires HighCpuAlert/LowMemoryAlert
notifications.

Client queries all three methods in a formatted table and listens for
threshold notifications. Test suite covers all RPCs, both notification
types, invalid method handling, and RunLoop dispatch mode (8 tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add disk-usage example with simulated volumes

Implement the disk-usage example under examples/macos/disk-usage/:

- disk_device.cpp: Simulated server with 3 volumes (/, /home, /tmp).
  Usage on / grows 1 GB every 3s; fires DiskSpaceLow when > 90%.
- disk_client.cpp: Interactive CLI with list, check <mount>, quit
  commands and bar-chart display. Prints notification alerts.
- disk_test.cpp: 8 Google Test cases covering GetVolumeCount,
  GetVolumeAt (valid/invalid), GetDiskUsage (known/unknown mount),
  DiskSpaceLow notification, UsedPercent consistency, RunLoop dispatch.
- CMakeLists.txt: Builds device, client, and test executables.
- README.md: IDL overview, build/run instructions, sample output.

fix: use local path loading for offline mode instead of snapshot_download

snapshot_download() caches model files but optimum-intel still makes an
API call (tree/main?recursive=True) that isn't cached. By saving the model

API calls entirely — the only reliable way to support offline mode with
the openvino backend.

commit.message:
fix: cache full HF Hub metadata for offline model loading

The SentenceTransformer() call alone doesn't cache HF Hub tree listings
and API metadata. At runtime with HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1, optimum-intel tries
to reach huggingface.co/api/models to discover openvino files, which
fails in offline mode.

Fix:
- Add snapshot_download() before SentenceTransformer() to cache the
  complete HF Hub repo including metadata, tree listings, and configs
- Add offline verification step that sets HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 and
  TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1, then loads the model — build fails fast if
  the cache is incomplete

Both torch and openvino variants are covered.

commit.message:
fix: YouTube upload quota throttling — split runs, cap uploads, backoff (#72)

- Split scheduled runs: EN at 06:00 UTC, HE at 12:00 UTC
- Cap at 6 uploads per run (configurable via --max-uploads)
- Add 60s delay between uploads to avoid rate limits
- Exponential backoff (30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 480s) on transient errors
- Detect uploadLimitExceeded/quotaExceeded as non-fatal: defer to next run
- Workflow exits 0 on quota hits (no false failures)
- Only hard-fail if non-quota errors AND zero successful uploads

Fixes run #9 failure where 6/12 videos hit uploadLimitExceeded.
